title: SQLite single-writer tactical mitigation (busy-timeout + read-only degrade + rebuild sentinel + rebuild sharding)
intent: Replace the hard-coded sqlite3.connect(timeout=120) calls in src/state/db.py with a configurable ZEUS_DB_BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS (default 30000ms per T0_SQLITE_POLICY); on sqlite3.OperationalError 'database is locked' the live cycle degrades to read-only-monitor for that cycle and increments db_write_lock_timeout_total; scripts/rebuild_calibration_pairs_v2.py refuses to start if .zeus/rebuild_lock.do_not_run_during_live exists; the rebuild splits its monolithic write transaction into bounded per-(city,metric) transactions so the writer-lock-hold duration is bounded.

asserted_invariants.text: src/state/db.py reads ZEUS_DB_BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS at module-load (or per-call) and applies the resulting timeout (in seconds) uniformly to BOTH sqlite3.connect surfaces today: line 40 (in _connect, used by get_trade_connection / get_world_connection / get_backtest_connection) AND line 349 (in get_connection used by ZEUS_DB_PATH legacy path). Default is 30 (=30000ms) per T0_SQLITE_POLICY.md.

Setting ZEUS_DB_BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS=5000 in the environment causes sqlite3.connect calls in db.py to use timeout=5.0 seconds; setting an unset/empty value falls back to 30. test_sqlite_busy_timeout.py asserts both branches with monkeypatched env.

On sqlite3.OperationalError where the message starts with 'database is locked', the live cycle path catches the error, increments db_write_lock_timeout_total, logs a structured ALERT-eligible event, and continues for that cycle in read-only mode (no write side effects). The daemon does NOT crash. test_db_write_timeout_does_not_crash_daemon synthesizes a long writer and asserts cycle returns without raising.

scripts/rebuild_calibration_pairs_v2.py raises SystemExit (or returns non-zero) at startup if .zeus/rebuild_lock.do_not_run_during_live exists in the repo root. The refusal happens BEFORE any DB connection is opened. test_rebuild_refuses_during_live asserts this with a tempdir-relative sentinel.

scripts/rebuild_calibration_pairs_v2.py commits per (city, temperature_metric) bucket rather than holding one savepoint across all cities; max single-transaction duration is bounded by one (city, metric) bucket size. test_rebuild_shards_transactions inspects the commit cadence either via a SQLite trace hook or by counting sqlite3.Connection.commit() calls.

round2_grep_verification.two_connect_sites: src/state/db.py:40 (in _connect) and src/state/db.py:349 (in get_connection) — confirmed at planner Round-1; still unchanged at Round-2 grep.
round2_grep_verification.no_existing_zeus_db_busy_timeout_ms: grep -rn ZEUS_DB_BUSY_TIMEOUT src/ scripts/ config/ → 0 matches at Round-2; T1E is the introduction.
round2_grep_verification.operational_error_uses_in_db_py: src/state/db.py:329, 1443, 1451, 1455, 1467, 1485, 1497, 1511, 1522, 1533, 1542, 1548, 1557, 1562 — these are existing OperationalError handlers, but ALL are for ALTER-TABLE / CREATE-TABLE-IF-NOT-EXISTS migrations, NOT for 'database is locked' busy timeouts. The new T1E catch is in the live cycle write path, which is at a different layer.
round2_grep_verification.rebuild_script_already_in_manifest: architecture/script_manifest.yaml:478 already registers rebuild_calibration_pairs_v2.py with class=repair, dangerous_if_run=true, target_db=state/zeus-world.db. T1E updates this row to add required_helpers (sentinel check) and required_tests (the new test files).
round2_grep_verification.sentinel_dir_present: Coordinator already created .zeus/rebuild_lock.do_not_run_during_live per LOCK_DECISION Amendment 2. T1E wires the script-side check that consumes the sentinel.

_planner_notes.loc_estimate_basis: src/state/db.py is 6118 lines. The T1E patch is small in db.py: ~30 lines (env read + apply to two connect sites + new exception-translate helper for the live-cycle catch). scripts/rebuild_calibration_pairs_v2.py: ~80 lines (sentinel check at top, then refactor the bulk write into a per-bucket loop with intermediate commits). Two new test files: ~150 lines each. Total ~410 LOC; the 380 estimate is conservative (some test code can reuse existing fixtures).
_planner_notes.cross_module_edges: 0 — T1E does not introduce a new module or import. It uses existing os.environ, sqlite3.OperationalError, and the existing sentinel file path constant.
_planner_notes.topology_admission: BLOCKED at planner time — generic profile does not admit src/state/db.py for write-intent edit. Resolution per LOCK_DECISION Amendment 4.
_planner_notes.k0_k1_classification: K0 STATE TRUTH — db.py is db_connection_schema_runtime per source_rationale.yaml. Critic should be opus. Verifier MUST reproduce the 'database is locked' synthetic-writer test.
coordinator_blockers: T0.2 RiskGuard still RUNNING (planner Round-1 amendment 1; coordinator confirmed escalation pending).
T0.5 SQLite policy: planner pre-filled 30000ms / pull-forward T2G in T0_SQLITE_POLICY.md; operator may override before T1E dispatch.
_planner_notes.predecessor_dependency: T1A (DDL single-source) MUST land first — T1E touches db.py and any DDL refactor T1A makes will conflict at the same file. T1F and T1BD do not directly conflict but they may have introduced the counter-sink module that T1E imports for db_write_lock_timeout_total.
_planner_notes.successor_constraint: T2G (DB physical isolation) is gated by T1E telemetry stability. db_write_lock_timeout_total nonzero in shadow runs blocks T3 corrected-live until T2G lands per T0_SQLITE_POLICY operator decision.
_planner_notes.closeout_drift_resolution: T2F-DOC-DRIFT-CLEANED: typed counter sink landed in T2F B1 (2026-05-05). All T1 phases share src/observability/counters.py. No per-phase DDL or schema drift outstanding.
